verdict.note: full loop through the real product (worktree dashboard → clone backend, Playwright-recorded): ok on the detail header hides the fresh row, '☑ 1 ok'd' chip reveals it settled, a newer reading brings it back unblessed; the dashboard click landed as clone-trunk commit 4ea70bcb

real spex CLI on a trunk-checkout clone: sign-off lands as a path-scoped trunk commit (2e0a08cf, no pre-commit gate); repeat idempotent; unmeasured scenario and governed session both refused loud; ls tags the blessed reading ☑

Production YATU on committed HEAD: fresh human-ok stayed hidden until the chip reveal; row and chip used the shared SVG check, the 17px certification ring had role=img + signer/time aria text, and no Unicode checkbox remained.

Disposable-clone YATU on committed HEAD: feed rows had zero write actions and detail had the sole button; sign-off 200 hid the fresh row, chip revealed a status-only SVG mark, and a newer CLI reading restored the unblessed row/detail button without transferring the ok.

real CLI on an isolated trunk checkout: all four eval-ok paths use the canonical eval noun; first write committed only the sidecar, repeat was idempotent, and no-eval/governed-session refusals stayed loud
